Era ( https://puck.news/the-end-of-the-art-worlds-arb-era/?utm_campaign=3DT=
he+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+=
SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustom=
er.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ). Alas, nascent media companies,
as I=E2=80=99ve learned from experience, are an eight-day-a-week job, and I=
 was grateful that my family was out playing in the yard so I could get som=
e work done.

As I moved through my punch list, I started making preparations to tape the=
 weekly Media Monday episode of The Power That Be ( https://puck.news/podca=
st_episode/media-monday-the-biden-finale-pelosis-mind-games/?utm_campaign=
=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstor=
y+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcu=
stomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ), my partner Peter Hamby=E2=80=99s=
 excellent daily podcast that serves as the inner sanctum of the Puck cinem=
atic universe. It seemed like a good idea for me to make some notes on disc=
ussion topics so
that we wouldn=E2=80=99t have to talk, yet again, about Joe Biden=E2=80=99s=
 battle with the media and his party over the fate of his presidency.

And then=E2=80=94as you can well imagine, dear reader=E2=80=94you know what=
 happened next. I heard my wife audibly gasp from down below and I picked u=
p my phone to find out that Biden had exited the race. I=E2=80=99m not a ne=
ws junkie, per se, but I am an information addict=E2=80=94there=E2=80=99s a=
n essential difference=E2=80=94and so I pinged Peter, who remains umbilical=
ly connected to Bidenworld, to find out what he was hearing and outline the=
 show. Then I hopped on the horn with John Heilemann, whose information on =
the unfolding Biden saga has been two or three steps ahead of anyone else=
=E2=80=99s reporting. Before Peter and I started taping, there was a more u=
rgent matter to sort out: Heilemann and I needed to figure out a plan for w=
hat he was going to publish that night for his Sunday evening column.

As I=E2=80=99ve noted in this space before,
editing is the art of elimination=E2=80=94and in many ways, that=E2=80=99s =
also one of our founding principles at Puck. There=E2=80=99s too much conte=
nt in the culture, and the vast majority is garbage. Our business, on the c=
ontrary, is predicated on extraordinary and thoughtful and stylish work, fi=
lled with news and insights you simply cannot find anywhere else, from auth=
ors you adore. And John, in many ways, is the culture=E2=80=99s foremost ex=
pert on presidential politics, the heir to Doris Kearns Goodwin and Richard=
 Ben Cramer. As we chatted, I was interested not only in what he was hearin=
g, but how he was processing it. We spoke for about 45 minutes. I wished I=
=E2=80=99d taped the conversation and simply transcribed it.

Later that evening, John pulled his thoughts together in the form of the ex=
cellent After Biden: Pelosi=E2=80=99s Power, Kamala=E2=80=99s Positioning &=
 What=E2=80=99s Next ( https://puck.news/after-biden-pelosis-power-kamalas-=
positioning-whats-next/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F2=
7%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_m=
edium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a=
 ). It ushered in an extraordinary week from our incredible political team.=
 After we taped The Powers That Be, Peter and I talked through the beats of=
 his brilliant story, Reintroducing Kamala ( https://puck.news/defining-kam=
ala-harris-will-be-crucial-for-both-campaigns/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory=
+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+=
%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_te=
rm=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ). Julia Ioffe captured the internationalist dimens=
ion in The Treaty of Harris=20
( https://puck.news/what-would-kamala-harris-foreign-policy-look-like/?utm_=
campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+=
Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_sou=
rce=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ), and Tara Palmeri surface=
d the collective soliloquy from Palm Beach with The Harris Reset & Hillbill=
y Blues ( https://puck.news/jd-vance-honeymoon-is-over/?utm_campaign=3DThe+=
Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUB=
SCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.=
io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ).

Forget Paris

The Biden announcement, and Harris=E2=80=99s subsequent fundraising conques=
ts, certainly animated the political corner of the ever-sprawling Puck univ=
erse. But the leitmotif of generational transition coursed through other po=
wer corners, too. In Tom Ford After Tom Ford=E2=80=A6 ( https://puck.news/t=
om-ford-after-tom-ford/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F2=
7%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_m=
edium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a=
 ), my partner Lauren Sherman exquisitely recounted the post-founder blues =
befalling a fashion and beauty icon. And in her subsequent masterstroke, Fa=
shion=E2=80=99s Red Scare & More Designer Musical Chairs ( https://puck.new=
s/chinas-muted-passion-for-fashion-hits-lvmh-kering-earnings/?utm_campaign=
=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstor=
y+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcu=
stomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ), she
articulated how a post-Z.I.R.P. panic is playing out inside various maisons=
=2E

And then, there was the apparent denouement of the NBA=E2=80=99s endless me=
dia rights auction, which has been well covered in Puck=E2=80=99s digital p=
ages. For its part, the NBA signaled from the outset of its negotiations th=
at the league wanted to move beyond purely linear partners and offer one pa=
ckage to a streamer. And yet in a world where the broadcast and cable busin=
ess increasingly hinge on live sports rights, the stakes seemed existential=
=2E Disney needed the NBA to launch its=
 two forthcoming streaming services. A=
mazon had been champing at the bit to get in on the action. And then there =
was WBD, whose TNT had carried league games since the =E2=80=9980s.

In How Zaz Lost the NBA ( https://puck.news/how-tnt-david-zaslav-lost-the-n=
ba/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_conten=
t=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action=
&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ), my partner John =
Ourand offers the incredibly unvarnished tale of how WBD flubbed the auctio=
n, and precisely when and why Comcast=E2=80=99s NBCU came in to steal its r=
ights. The story, which presaged WBD=E2=80=99s lawsuit against the league (=
and its bizarre P.R. offensive, as excellently portrayed ( https://puck.new=
s/a-pr-crisis-for-the-david-zaslav-era/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBS=
CRIBERS+%287%2F27%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%287%2F=
19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6=
c60617da42abd12a ) by Dylan Byers), is about so much more than sports and m=
edia, of course. It=E2=80=99s a metaphor for the profound, even staggering,=
 changes afoot in our industry and
economy and the powerlessness of even the most mighty executive to overcome=
 them. It is, in some many ways, the story of our time=E2=80=94and, as you =
well know by now, precisely what you should expect from Puck.

Have a great weekend,
Jon
